---
name: 3.chubb-case-study-run-migration
description: >
  Use to apply EF Core database migrations for the Chubb APAC Policy Management Platform.
  Covers setting the connection string, running all pending migrations, verifying applied migrations,
  and rolling back if needed. Targets SQL Server running locally or via Docker.
---

# Run EF Core Migrations — Chubb APAC Policy Management Platform

## Prerequisites

- .NET 8 SDK installed
- `dotnet-ef` global tool installed:
  ```powershell
  dotnet tool install --global dotnet-ef
  # or update if already installed
  dotnet tool update --global dotnet-ef
  ```
- SQL Server reachable (local install or Docker — see docker-compose.yml)

---

## Step 1 — Set the Connection String

Update the connection string in
`src/backend/Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api/appsettings.Development.json`:

```json
"ConnectionStrings": {
   "DefaultConnection": "Server=.;Database=ChubbPolicyManagement;Trusted_Connection=True;TrustServerCertificate=True;"
}
```

> **Never commit real credentials.** For CI/CD, override via environment variable:
> ```powershell
> $env:ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection = "Server=...;Database=...;..."
> ```

---

## Step 2 — Start SQL Server (Docker, if not running locally)

```powershell
cd "c:\Users\VishwanathaReddyKs\OneDrive - EPAM\Coding\AI\Case Study"
docker compose up db -d
```

Wait a few seconds for SQL Server to become healthy before running migrations.

---

## Step 3 — List Pending Migrations

Check which migrations have not yet been applied:

```powershell
cd "c:\Users\VishwanathaReddyKs\OneDrive - EPAM\Coding\AI\Case Study\src"

dotnet ef migrations list `
  --project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Infrastructure `
  --startup-project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api
```

Expected output (all three should show as pending on a fresh database):

```
20260608084254_InitialSchema
20260608084319_AddPolicyIndexes
20260608084438_SeedPolicies
```

---

## Step 4 — Apply All Migrations

```powershell
cd "c:\Users\VishwanathaReddyKs\OneDrive - EPAM\Coding\AI\Case Study\src"

dotnet ef database update `
  --project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Infrastructure `
  --startup-project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api
```

This applies the three migrations in order:

| Migration | What it does |
|---|---|
| `InitialSchema` | Creates the `Policies` table with all columns |
| `AddPolicyIndexes` | Adds performance indexes for filtering/sorting |
| `SeedPolicies` | Inserts 210 realistic APAC seed records (idempotent) |

---

## Rollback Commands

### Roll back to a specific migration

```powershell
dotnet ef database update AddPolicyIndexes `
  --project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Infrastructure `
  --startup-project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api
```

### Roll back all migrations (drops all tables)

```powershell
dotnet ef database update 0 `
  --project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Infrastructure `
  --startup-project backend\Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api
```

---

## Verify the Database

After applying migrations, connect to SQL Server and run:

```sql
-- Confirm table exists
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM [Policies];          -- should return 210

-- Check status distribution
SELECT [Status], COUNT(*) AS Total
FROM [Policies]
GROUP BY [Status];

-- Check indexes
SELECT name FROM sys.indexes WHERE object_id = OBJECT_ID('Policies');
```

---

## Troubleshooting

| Error | Fix |
|---|---|
| `Cannot open server` | Check SQL Server is running and the port/credentials in the connection string are correct |
| `Login failed for user 'sa'` | SA login may be disabled — enable it or use a different SQL account |
| `Your startup project doesn't reference Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design` | The package is already added to `Chubb.PolicyManagement.Api.csproj` — run `dotnet restore` |
| Migration already applied | Safe to re-run — EF Core skips already-applied migrations; seed is idempotent |
